proven.claim: Ordering constraint: args is provably non-empty at line 22.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 22
; Ordering constraint: args is provably non-empty at line 22.
; The help guard at line 16 exits when args.length === 0; since we reached line 22 that guard was false.
; PROVE: args_length > 0 AND includes_version = 1 are both true at line 22.
(declare-const args_length Int)
(declare-const includes_version Int)   ; 1 = true, 0 = false
; Code transition: guard at line 16 exits when args.length === 0; we passed it → args_length > 0
(assert (> args_length 0))
; Code transition: line 20 checks args.includes("--version"); we entered the branch → true
(assert (= includes_version 1))
; Negation: either args is empty or --version absent at line 22
(assert (or (<= args_length 0) (= includes_version 0)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: --version is checked via Array.includes(), which scans ALL positions, not just args[0].
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 22
; --version is checked via Array.includes(), which scans ALL positions, not just args[0].
; A user who passes "neurallog analyze src/foo.ts --version" intends to run analyze,
; but the version flag at position 2 short-circuits dispatch entirely and silently discards the command.
(declare-const version_flag_position Int)    ; 0-indexed position of "--version" in args
(declare-const command_position Int)         ; position of command name — always 0
(declare-const a_valid_command_present Int)  ; 1 = args[0] is a known command like "analyze"
(declare-const command_dispatched Int)       ; 1 = the switch arm executed
; Code transition: command_position is always 0 (args[0] is the dispatch token)
(assert (= command_position 0))
; Code transition: includes("--version") fires regardless of flag position
; version flag is after the command (user passed "analyze src/foo.ts --version")
(assert (> version_flag_position command_position))
; Code transition: args[0] = "analyze" → a valid command is present
(assert (= a_valid_command_present 1))
; Code transition: line 20 guard fires because includes("--version") = true → process.exit(0)
; The switch is never reached → command_dispatched = 0
(assert (= command_dispatched 0))
; Violation: a valid command was present but was never executed
(assert (= a_valid_command_present 1))
(assert (= command_dispatched 0))
(check-sat)
